data.title: Nikkei Falls 2.1%, Dragged by Chip, Heavy Industrial Stocks — Market Talk
data.description: Japanese stocks are lower due to persistent concerns about higher energy costs and a shortage of petrochemical products amid the Middle East conflict. Chip and heavy industry stocks are leading declines. Kioxia Holdings is down 6.9%, SoftBank Group is 4.2% lower and Mitsubishi Heavy Industries is down 4.0%. The dollar is at 159.90 yen, compared with Y159.75 as of Monday's Tokyo stock market close. Investors are closely watching developments in Iran and any Japanese government steps to address the shortage of energy and petrochemical products. The Nikkei Stock Average is down 2.1% at 50789.02. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

data.title: JGB Futures Edge Lower Amid Rising Oil Prices — Market Talk
data.description: JGB futures edge lower in the morning Tokyo session amid rising oil prices. The summary of opinions at the Bank of Japan's March meeting suggests discussion was skewed to concern over the risk of higher oil prices driving faster inflation, SMBC Nikko Securities' Lisa Mochizuki says in a research report. "We continue to see an April hike as the base case," the junior analyst says. "Since the situation in the Middle East intensified, the JGB yield curve has bear-steepened. If the BOJ prioritizes inflation risks, then concerns about runaway long-term inflation could ease," Mochizuki adds. The benchmark 10-year JGB futures are 0.12 yen lower at 130.22 yen. (ronnie.harui@wsj.com)

data.title: Japan Industrial Production Slipped 2.1% In February
data.description:
TOKYO (dpa-AFX) - Industrial output in Japan was down a seasonally adjusted 2.1 percent on month in February, the Ministry of Economy, Trade and Industry said on Tuesday.

That missed forecasts for a decline of 2.0 percent following the 4.3 percent increase in January.

On a yearly basis, industrial production was up 0.3 percent.

Upon the release of the data, the METI maintained its assessment of industrial production, saying that it continues to fluctuate indecisively.

According to the METI's forecast, industrial production is expected to rise 3.8 percent in March and 3.3 percent in April.

data.title: Japan's Industrial Output Declined in February; Mideast Tensions Cloud Outlook
data.description:
(23:50 GMT) *Japan Feb Indus Output -2.1% on Month; Mkt Expected -2.0%
(23:51 GMT) *Japan Feb Inventory-Shipments Ratio +2.3% on Month
(23:51 GMT) *Japan Feb Shipments -1.6% on Month
(23:52 GMT) *Japan Feb Inventories +0.3% on Month
(23:52 GMT) *Japan Cos Forecast Mar Indus Output +3.8% on Month
(23:53 GMT) *Japan Cos Forecast Apr Indus Output +3.3% on Month
(00:06 GMT) Japan's Industrial Output Declined in February; Mideast Tensions Cloud Outlook

By Megumi Fujikawa

TOKYO--Japan's industrial production declined in February, a pullback from the pre-holiday demand boost in the previous month.

Industrial output fell 2.1% in February, following January's 4.3% increase, government data showed Tuesday. That compared with the 2.0% drop expected in a poll of economists by data provider Quick.

The outlook remains uncertain as it is still unclear to what extent the Middle East conflict will weigh on the economy. If tensions persist, that could disrupt supply chains further and lead to a weakening of factory activity.

Japanese companies expect output to rise 3.8% in March and increase 3.3% in April, the data showed.

Write to Megumi Fujikawa at megumi.fujikawa@wsj.com

data.title: Australia's Collins Foods to transfer 20 Taco Bell outlets to Yum Brand-linked group
data.description: Australia's Collins Foods said on Tuesday it would hand over 20 of its 27 Taco Bell restaurants in the country to an affiliate of Yum Brands and Restaurant Brands, bringing it closer to a full exit from the loss-making business.

data.title: Tokyo Overall Inflation Slows To 1.4% On Year In March
data.description:
TOKYO (dpa-AFX) - Overall consumer prices in the Tokyo region of Japan were up 1.4 percent on month in March, the Ministry of Economy, Trade and Industry said on Tuesday.

That was below forecasts for 1.5 percent and down from 1.6 percent in February.

Core CPI was up 1.7 percent - again shy of expectations for 1.8 percent, which would have been unchanged from the previous month.

data.title: Iron Ore Prices Get Support From Higher Shipping Costs, Firm Demand — Market Talk
data.description: Rising shipping costs amid ongoing geopolitical tensions are providing support to iron-ore prices, says Morgan Stanley. Demand has also been relatively resilient, MS says. "Though inventories remain elevated, incremental tons have been absorbed for now," the bank says. "We expect higher energy prices have temporarily lifted marginal cost support, helping to underpin pricing." Low-cost producers are best positioned amid an energy market shock, given they are typically more fuel efficient, says MS. Spot iron ore traded at $108.50/metric ton Monday, gaining 0.4%, according to Platts, part of S&P Global Energy. (rhiannon.hoyle@wsj.com; @RhiannonHoyle)

data.title: Key facts: XRP faces liquidity drop, large flows and ledger reforms
data.description:
• Order-book analysis for XRP (XRPUSD) shows a rise to $3.60 could liquidate over $320M in shorts, while a fall to $0.39 could wipe about $130M in longs, highlighting asymmetric exposure.1
  • XRP liquidity fell: XRP Ledger AMM pools ~$1.9M, DEX liquidity down from ~$280B to ~$104.2B, whale-to-exchange transfers rose in Feb–Mar; analysts cite downside targets near $0.90–$0.75.2
  • XRP (XRPUSD) saw $15.8M inflows last week, one of few crypto assets to get net institutional/managed fund inflows while others had outflows.3
  • March 30, 2026: XRP Ledger recorded 624.6M daily payments, up 410.7% from ~122M the prior day; XRP traded near $1.30.4
  • Proposal to add issuer controls (freeze, clawback), audit-on-demand, and hybrid public/confidential balances to XRP Ledger. Changes aim to support private digital assets, bank stablecoins and CBDCs.5
  • Ripple set to release 1 billion XRP from escrow on April 1. Traders note Ripple often relocks much of monthly releases within 48 hours; no price commentary provided.6
  • XRP (XRPUSD) 21‑day EMA crossed above 200‑day EMA, then dropped ~14%. Analyst projects possible bottom ~Apr 16. Immediate support $1.15; further supports $0.93, $0.73, $0.42; upside $1.60–$3.027
  • Former Ripple CTO David Schwartz said higher XRP price cuts XRP needed for equal-value transfers, which could lower liquidity strain and slippage in Ripple’s On-Demand Liquidity (ODL) model.8
  • XRP used in Singapore pilot: Ripple and Unloq will trial cross‑border trade finance on the XRP Ledger using RLUSD to automate payments and record settlement events for transparency.9

data.title: Key facts: Bitcoin draws institutional inflows; on-chain shows weakness
data.description:
• March 2026: U.S. spot Bitcoin ETFs saw strong monthly net inflows after prior outflows; weekly flows mixed with notable early-month inflows and a late-month outflow as institutions continued buying.12
  • MicroStrategy bought ~18,000 BTC on March 9 and ~22,000 BTC on March 16 during a mid-Feb–late-Mar 2026 accumulation. Purchases were partly equity-funded; BTC buying paused Mar 23–29 due to financing.3
  • Bitcoin (BTCUSD) logged $194M in net outflows last week and $964M net inflows year-to-date, showing recent capital exits amid larger cumulative institutional inflows this year.4
  • Basel SCO60 sets a 1,250% risk weight for unbacked crypto like Bitcoin. US regulators haven’t clarified whether they’ll adopt SCO60 or how Bitcoin exposures will be treated.5
  • MicroStrategy Executive Chairman Michael Saylor said the company intends to buy bitcoin every quarter and is not planning to sell, signaling continued accumulation beyond its recent pause.6
  • On-chain data: short-term holder realized price fell to ~$83,200. Broader realized price and CVDD point to support zones near $50,000 or below for BTCUSD trading.7
  • Analyst Willy Woo cites on-chain models: Realized Price ~ $54,200 and CVDD ~ $45,500, suggesting cycle support band roughly $46,000–$54,000 for BTCUSD based on past bear-market behavior.8
  • BTCUSD faced about $375M in total liquidations: roughly $241M longs and $134M shorts. Largest single reported liquidation was a $9.8M BTCUSD order on Bybit.9
  • Bitcoin on-chain data: long-term holder profitability plunged from 58% to 3% in about 140 days; LTH net unrealized P/L stayed just above zero during that stretch.10
  • BTCUSD finds near-term support mid-$60k, could slide toward $60k if selling persists; March monthly close status remained unresolved at the time.11

data.title: Key facts: Aave $1T Volume; TVL $23.5B; v3 on OKX; KelpDAO adds rsETH
data.description:
• Aave (AAVEUSD) hit $1T cumulative lending volume. TVL $23.5B; $6.2M revenue past 30 days; $40.4B net deposits across 20+ chains.1
  • Aave v3 launched on OKX’s X Layer. OKX users can lend, earn auto-compounding interest, and borrow via Aave. Traders should track TVL, borrowing rates, fees and asset listings for AAVEUSD.23
  • KelpDAO integrated Aave V4, adding rsETH via a spoke and setting e-mode parameters for rsETH, noting early support for LST/LRT use cases on Aave V4.4

data.title: AIC Mines Reports Increases to Eloise and Jericho Copper Resources and Ore Reserves
data.description:
AIC Mines has updated the mineral resource and ore reserve estimates for its Eloise copper mine and Jericho copper project in North Queensland.

The Eloise mineral resource increased by 10% to 31.2 million tonnes grading 2% copper and 0.4 grams per tonne gold for 631,800t copper (up 12%) and 445,800 ounces gold (up 9%), driven primarily by resource extension and infill drilling that extended mineralisation within inferred resources in the Deeps and Lens 6 deposits.

Mineral resource additions of 748,000t at 3.7% copper consisted of 205,000t at 2.5% in the Upper Zone (Lens 5 and Lode 42) at the Elrose-Levuka South deposit, 523,000t at 4.3% copper in the Deeps Lower Zone (Lenses 2, 3, 4 and 6), and 20,000t at 1.6% copper from end-of-period stockpiles.

The mine’s ore reserves rose 14% to 10.2Mt grading 1.9% copper and 0.4g/t gold for 191,000t copper (up 10%) and 139,600oz gold (up 10%).

Jericho Extensional Drilling

Resource extension drilling focused on the northern end of the Jericho deposit extended the strike of the indicated mineral resource by 600 metres to the north and added tonnages at the Matilda North, Jolly, Tucker, and Swagman shoots.

Jericho’s total mineral resources increased by 15% to 22.1Mt grading 2.0% copper and 0.4g/t gold containing 444,800t copper (up 17%) and 275,800oz gold (up 12%).

Ore reserves also rose, up 15% to 7.1Mt at 1.8% copper and 0.4g/t gold containing 125,400t copper (up 16%) and 125,400oz gold (up 16%).

Mineral resource additions of 2.9Mt included 339,000t at 2.2% copper from the new J0 lens 100m west of J1, 1.15Mt at 2.8% copper after delineation and infill drilling of the Jolly shoot extended the J1 resource by 600m along strike, and 1.42Mt at 1.7% copper from updated modelling and estimation parameters.

Underground Development

Recent development on the Jericho underground access drive at the J1 lens confirmed that the mineralisation style and geotechnical conditions are consistent with the project’s geological model, resulting in material reductions to mining and ramp-up risk.

The access drive was completed in February, four months ahead of schedule, having progressed a total 2,350m from the 1065 Level on the Eloise decline (125m below surface).

AIC is now working on completion of the JS3 ventilation shaft at the Jolly shoot, which is considered to be the next critical-path item for Jericho’s development.

JS3 is scheduled to be operational by mid-year to allow mining to commence at Jolly.

Significant Resource Growth

AIC managing director Aaron Colleran said the growth in resources and reserves across both projects had been significant.

“Resource definition and extension drilling in the last field season has increased resources and improved reserves at Eloise and Jericho,” he said.

“This again highlights the quality of these deposits and the strong geological, geophysical, geochemical, and structural understanding that our exploration and geology teams have developed

“It is a great time to own a copper mine," Mr Colleran added.

"With copper being a crucial component in electrical wiring – and, hence, electrification – its value is rising steadily and set to rise further, making copper mining operations more profitable than ever.”

data.title: PlasCred Circular Innovations Inc. Announces Non-Brokered Private Placement Under Listed Issuer Financing Exemption
data.description:
Calgary, Alberta--(Newsfile Corp. - March 30, 2026) - PlasCred Circular Innovations Inc. (FSE: XV2) ("PlasCred" or the "Company"), is pleased to announce a non-brokered private placement pursuant to the listed issuer financing exemption (the "Offering") of National Instrument 45-106 – Prospectus Exemptions ("NI 45-106"). Proceeds from the Offering will be used to advance development of the Company's Neos commercial facility including detailed engineering, permitting, procurement of long-lead equipment, and for general working capital.

The Offering will consist of up to 29,411,765 units (each, a "Unit") at a price of $0.17 per Unit for gross proceeds of up to $5,000,000, with a minimum offering size of $2,500,000. Each Unit is comprised of one common share ("Common Share") in the capital of the Company and one common share purchase warrant (the "Warrant"). Each Warrant will entitle the holder to purchase one Common Share at a price of $0.22 per Common Share for a period of 36 months following the closing date of the Offering (the "Closing Date"). The Warrants will be subject to an early expiration provision whereby if, during a period of 10 consecutive trading days between the Closing Date and the expiry of the Warrants, the daily volume-weighted average trading price of the Common Shares on the CSE (or such other stock exchange where the majority of the trading volume occurs) exceeds $0.40 for each of those 10 consecutive days, the Company may, within 30 days of such an occurrence, give written notice to the holders, following which notice the holders of the Warrants will have 30 days to exercise their Warrants. Any Warrants issued pursuant to the Offering are subject to a restriction on exercise expiring 61 days from the Closing Date.

Subject to compliance with applicable regulatory requirements and in accordance with NI 45-106, the Units will be offered for sale to purchasers resident in all of the provinces of Canada with the exception of Québec pursuant to the listed issuer financing exemption under Part 5A of NI 45-106, as amended by Coordinated Blanket Order 45-935 – Exemptions from Certain Conditions of the Listed Issuer Financing Exemption (the "Listed Issuer Financing Exemption"). The securities issuable from the sale of the Units are expected to be immediately freely tradeable in accordance with applicable Canadian securities legislation if sold to purchasers resident in Canada.

The Company may pay finders' fees to eligible persons who introduce subscribers to the Offering, consisting of: (i) a cash commission equal to 7% of the gross proceeds raised from the sale of Units to subscribers introduced by such person; and (ii) the issuance of non-transferable broker warrants entitling the holder thereof to acquire that number of Common Shares equal to 7% of the number of Units sold to subscribers introduced by such person, exercisable at a price of $0.22 per Common Share for a period of 36 months following the Closing Date. The completion of the Offering is subject to the receipt of all required regulatory approvals, including approval from the CSE. The Offering is expected to close on or about April 16, 2026.

There is an offering document (the "Offering Document") related to the Offering that can be accessed under the Company's profile at www.sedarplus.ca. Prospective investors should read this Offering Document before making an investment decision.

About PlasCred Circular Innovations Inc.

PlasCred is an Alberta-based company developing an advanced plastic recycling facility. The Company's engineered, modular platform converts mixed plastic waste into refined hydrocarbon condensate for use in virgin plastic production, petrochemical feedstock, and upstream energy applications. For further information on PlasCred, visit our website at www.PlasCred.com.

ON BEHALF OF THE BOARD

Troy Lupul – President & CEO

Contact Information

For more information please contact:

PlasCred Circular Innovations Inc.

Troy Lupul

Email: IR@plascred.com

Forward-looking Statements

Forward-looking statements in this release include, but are not limited to: closing of the Offering on the terms described herein or at all; the expected closing date of the Offering; and the use of proceeds of the Offering,. Forward-looking statements are based on management's current assumptions and expectations, which are subject to known and unknown risks, uncertainties, and other factors that may cause actual results, performance, or achievements to differ materially from those expressed or implied. Such risks and uncertainties include, without limitation: construction, commissioning, and start-up risks; cost overruns; delays or disruptions in the supply chain; ability to achieve and maintain nameplate capacity at scale; changes in feedstock availability, composition, or pricing; fluctuations in commodity prices and foreign exchange rates; failure of counterparties to perform under offtake, financing, or strategic agreements; changes in applicable laws, regulations, or EPR requirements; inability to secure or maintain permits; adverse changes in market demand for advanced recycling products; evolving ESG reporting standards; technology performance or reliability issues; and general economic, political, and capital market conditions. A discussion of these and other factors that may affect future results is contained in the Company's continuous disclosure filings available under its profile on SEDAR+ at www.sedarplus.ca. Forward-looking statements are not guarantees of future performance, and readers should not place undue reliance on them. Except as required by applicable securities laws, the Company undertakes no obligation to revise or update any forward-looking statements to reflect new events, circumstances, or otherwise.

The Canadian Securities Exchange (operated by CNSX Markets Inc.) has neither approved nor disapproved of the contents of this press release.

data.title: Primaris REIT Announces AGM and Financial Results Release Date, Webcast, and Conference Call
data.description:
TORONTO--(BUSINESS WIRE)--March 30, 2026--

Primaris Real Estate Investment Trust ("Primaris" or the "Trust") will be releasing its financial results for the quarter ended March 31, 2026, on Wednesday, April 29, 2026, after the market closes. On Thursday, April 30, 2026, Primaris will host its hybrid 2026 Annual General Meeting, and will host a conference call and webcast presentation to discuss the quarterly financial and operating results.

Hybrid Annual General Meeting:

Date: Thursday, April 30, 2026, at 10:00 a.m. (ET)

Virtual: To attend virtually please go to the Investor Relations section on Primaris' website or click here.

In-Person: Blake, Cassels, & Graydon LLP, 199 Bay Street, 40th Floor, Suite 4000, Toronto, Canada, M5L 1A9.

The Annual General Meeting will be accessible for replay until April 30, 2027 on the Investor Relations section of the website.

About Primaris Real Estate Investment Trust

Primaris is Canada's only enclosed shopping centre focused REIT, with ownership interests in leading enclosed shopping centres located in growing Canadian markets. The current portfolio totals 15.2 million square feet, valued at approximately $5.2 billion at Primaris' share. Economies of scale are achieved through its fully internal, vertically integrated, full-service national management platform. Primaris is very well-capitalized and is exceptionally well positioned to take advantage of market opportunities at an extraordinary moment in the evolution of the Canadian retail property landscape.

For more information: TSX: PMZ.UN www.primarisreit.com www.sedarplus.ca
